// ==== Sales/infrastructure/Behaviors.ts ====
// <auto-generated/>
import { PipelineBehavior, ValidationError, Validator } from '../../infrastructure-runtime';
import { IUnitOfWork } from '../abstractions/IUnitOfWork';

/** Validation behavior: runs every supplied validator before the handler, rejecting with ValidationError on failure. With no validators it is a pass-through (Assumption 4). */
export function validationBehavior<TRequest, TResponse>(
  validators: readonly Validator<TRequest>[] = [],
): PipelineBehavior<TRequest, TResponse> {
  return (request, next) => {
    const errors = validators.flatMap((validator) => validator.validate(request));
    if (errors.length > 0) {
      return Promise.reject(new ValidationError(errors));
    }
    return next();
  };
}

/** Transaction behavior: commits the unit of work after a successful handler. */
export function transactionBehavior<TRequest, TResponse>(
  unitOfWork: IUnitOfWork,
): PipelineBehavior<TRequest, TResponse> {
  return (request, next) =>
    next().then((response) => unitOfWork.saveChangesAsync().then(() => response));
}

// ==== Sales/infrastructure/SalesInfrastructure.ts ====
// <auto-generated/>
import { PipelineBehavior } from '../../infrastructure-runtime';
import { IUnitOfWork } from '../abstractions/IUnitOfWork';
import { ICustomerRepository } from '../repositories/ICustomerRepository';
import { IOrderRepository } from '../repositories/IOrderRepository';
import { transactionBehavior, validationBehavior } from './Behaviors';
import { CustomerRepository } from './CustomerRepository';
import { OrderRepository } from './OrderRepository';
import { UnitOfWork } from './UnitOfWork';

/** The assembled Sales infrastructure: repositories, the unit of work, the command-pipeline behaviors. */
export interface SalesInfrastructure {
  readonly orders: IOrderRepository;
  readonly customers: ICustomerRepository;
  readonly unitOfWork: IUnitOfWork;
  readonly behaviors: readonly PipelineBehavior<unknown, unknown>[];
}

/** Composition root for the Sales context's infrastructure — the idiomatic analogue of C#'s AddSalesInfrastructure. Wires the in-memory defaults; inject persistent stores to productionize. */
export function createSalesInfrastructure(): SalesInfrastructure {
  const orders = new OrderRepository();
  const customers = new CustomerRepository();
  const unitOfWork = new UnitOfWork(orders, customers);
  const behaviors: readonly PipelineBehavior<unknown, unknown>[] = [
    validationBehavior(),
    transactionBehavior(unitOfWork),
  ];
  return { orders, customers, unitOfWork, behaviors };
}
